goal: 调整 phase1 conflicts 只报告 proposed 协作冲突

summary.title: Limit phase1 conflicts to proposed intents
summary.what: Changed the phase1 conflict surfaces so ordinary conflict pairs are only produced against proposed intents. SealSubmit, sync auto-check, and CheckPrepare now skip merged history when producing ConflictPair results or phase2 check tasks. Added tests that prove merged history no longer appears as a seal conflict or CheckPrepare task, while proposed overlaps still warn and remain advisory.
summary.why: Phase1 conflicts are meant to catch active collaboration overlap, not report every historical intent that touched the same file. Merged intents remain useful context through retrieval and hub history, but treating them as conflicts makes central files noisy and obscures real in-flight conflicts.
summary.user_goal: 调整 phase1 conflicts 只报告 proposed 协作冲突

decisions.chose: Restrict phase1 ConflictPair generation to proposed remote intents.
decisions.rationale: Proposed intents represent active or review-pending collaboration risk. Merged intents are historical context unless a future semantic guard explicitly checks anti_patterns or abandoned/superseded lineage.

decisions.chose: Apply the same proposed-only rule to CheckPrepare phase1 tasks.
decisions.rationale: Phase2 check tasks are spawned from phase1 suspicious pairs, so they should share the same collaboration-conflict scope.

decisions.chose: Do not add a new merged-intent warning type in this change.
decisions.rationale: Merged history needs a different semantic path based on anti_patterns, abandoned/superseded lineage, or explicit design principles. File-overlap scoring alone is too weak for that purpose.

rejected.alternative: Keep merged intents in ConflictPair and tune the threshold higher
rejected.reason: Threshold tuning would still label historical file overlap as conflict and remain noisy for central files.

rejected.alternative: Delete merged history from all phase1-adjacent surfaces
rejected.reason: Merged history is still valuable context; the change only removes it from ordinary collaboration-conflict warnings.

risks: True violations of historical merged decisions will no longer surface through generic phase1 ConflictPair output; they need the retrieval/anti_pattern path today and a dedicated semantic warning path later.

Seal was prepared with a pre-existing untracked .mainline/hub/ directory, so the seal audit marks evidence_complete=false even though the committed code diff is limited to conflict/check code and tests.

anti_patterns.what: Using shared-file overlap with merged intents as ordinary phase1 conflict evidence
anti_patterns.why: Merged intents are historical context, not active collaboration conflicts. File overlap against history is too noisy and should be surfaced through context/history unless a semantic rule is actually violated.

followups: Design a separate historical-intent warning surface for anti_pattern, abandoned-approach, and superseded-decision violations instead of overloading ConflictPair.

architectural_claims: Phase1 ConflictPair represents active proposed-intent collaboration overlap, not generic merged-history relevance.
Merged intent history should be consumed as context unless a dedicated semantic violation path flags it.
behavioral_changes: SealSubmit no longer reports conflicts against merged intents.
Sync auto-check no longer caches ConflictPair entries against merged remote intents.
CheckPrepare no longer creates phase2 tasks for merged-history overlap.
